feedback.text: I am a 40+ gamer and have seen plenty during my gaming life. As of the past 20 years things have started to go down a dangerous slippery slope with unfinished games on release (riddled with bugs that often break the game completely), micro-transactions and plenty of copy-paste sequels. I am quite tired of the games industry (mainly AAA) taking us gamers for granted, trying to milk every cent from us with Fear of Missing Out (FOMO) content. It started somewhat innocently with Bethesdas Micro-transaction shop, where you could spend money on various skins for your mounts. Jagex implemented their Squeel of Fortune to RuneScape back in 2011-ish. It basically meant you paid to play a game outside of the actual game, to try win experience lamps. These lamps allowed the character to level up skills without the gamer doing anything inside the actual game. Lately, game companies have started to shut down games, mostly live services, because its no longer a profitable endevour. They have basically sold something to a customer, taken their money, later shut down their services but without refunding the purchase, claiming you need to get used to not owning your games (written by EA games). Many of them offer no option for gamers to start their own servers to keep playing the game they purchased. Some even demand that once the game company no longer supports their sold game, gamers should destroy all copies, even physical copies, that they purchased. Another issue that has come to light is how card companies like VISA, Mastercard, and payment option PayPal have been targeted by a feminist grassroots group to put pressure on Steam to delist games they find distasteful. No matter the content of the game, it is not up to the card companies and other payment options to decide what adult gamers spend their money on. This is also a very dangerous path to take as it could set precedent. If enough people object loudly enough against, for instance adult romances in games (Baldurs Gate 3, Mass Effect, Witcher 3 etc), then it could lead to these games being delisted and become unplayable if you only own a digital copy. I for one own these types of games and would be royally upset if those were taken from me without compensation. Thank you for reading this.
